subject: Warp Crashing
body:
Hi Dimitry,

I've been having trouble with Warp crashing recently. I have a large data 
collection that is currently on-going. Part way through the data collection 
our RAID array filled up causing SerialEM to write out tif files without 
any data which I think are causing the program to crash. I've removed the 
bad tif files from the Warp directly and their associated xml files but I'm 
still getting intermittent crashes. I notice when I start Warp after the 
crash there is a single "Outdated" image that I imagine might be causing 
the issue. The program does not crash right away when I start processing 
again. Is their an easy way to identify the outdated image (it's a large 
collection so it's difficult to see the individual yellow dot). How does 
the program currently deal with "bad" files that it is trying to process? I 
imagine if I restart a new directly with only the good images it'll work 
fine as in the past but I wanted to share this information in case it is 
useful for future releases. If you have any suggestions I'd be happy to try 
them out. 

Cheers,
Evan
